user_query:
Please perform a MISRA C:2012 compliance review of the embedded C code in input/ecu_module.txt and produce two deliverables:
1) A detailed violation report at output/report.md using the exact violation block and summary format specified in the prompt below. For each violation, include: MISRA rule number, category (Mandatory/Required/Advisory), ASIL level (A–D), severity, location (with line number and brief description), the exact non-compliant line(s), a plain-English explanation, and a ready-to-use MISRA-compliant replacement. After listing all violations, include the required REVIEW SUMMARY block with totals by category and ASIL and an Overall compliance status.
2) A MISRA-compliant corrected version of the module saved to output/fixed_module.txt. In the corrected code, eliminate dynamic memory allocation entirely (no malloc/calloc/realloc/free), remove any use of goto, ensure all switch statements include a default clause and each case ends with break/return, use fixed-width integer types for all parameters/returns/locals (e.g., uint8_t/uint16_t/etc.), end if-else if chains with an else, avoid multiple early returns where practicable (prefer a single explicit return at the end for non-void functions), and ensure every non-void function returns on all paths. Do not introduce recursion or variadic arguments.
Additionally, write a machine-readable summary to output/summary.json with this structure: {"total_violations": <int>, "counts": {"Mandatory": <int>, "Required": <int>, "Advisory": <int>}, "asil": {"A": <int>, "B": <int>, "C": <int>, "D": <int>, "Not mapped": <int>}, "rules": ["<rule numbers e.g., 15.1>", ...]}. Make sure the rules list includes at least 15.1 and 21.3.
Note: Adhere to MISRA C:2012 and map each violation to an ASIL per ISO 26262. Read only from input/ and write all outputs under output/.
